approach: Second authoritative retrieval and one non-overlapping full fail-closed order-42 corpus gate

strategy.mechanism: Hash-linked raw HTTPS provenance gates two exact evaluators, three full-adjacency parser paths, target nauty complementation, structural signatures, and canonical labeling.

hypothesis: A second direct authoritative retrieval reproduces the frozen McKay bytes and the supplied 328 records plus their 328 complements pass every exact control gate.
discriminating_test: Require an immutable direct HTTP/TLS/body capture with no inferred headers, then run exactly one full target gate and stop on the first provenance, byte, parser, evaluator, complement, structural, or canonical mismatch.
summary: A newly live network path returned HTTP/2 200 from McKay's authoritative URL. The server omitted Content-Type, so provenance schema v2 was implemented to preserve that absence and link the admitted corpus to raw header, body, curl-transfer, and experiment captures. The second body exactly matched the first body and frozen 47,888-byte SHA-256. The single full gate passed on 328 source records and 328 complements. No completeness, witness, upper-bound, or exact-value claim is made; 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46 is unchanged.
rationale: The direct body/header capture closes the recorded provenance blocker without inventing a response field. Two materially different exact checkers, a third parser, nauty reference tools, published structural controls, semantic mutations, and exact agreement with the prior host run support the supplied-packet claim at its stated scope.
claims: The authoritative corpus body was independently retrieved twice with identical 47,888 bytes and SHA-256 067902e853d87b49bcef0d1d4c0e3bbadd238ee18bc65341b079a3ca4780eccb.
The authoritative HTTP/2 200 response omitted Content-Type; no media type is inferred in the accepted provenance.

For exactly the supplied 328 order-42 records and their 328 derived complements, both exact checkers found zero 5-cliques and zero independent 5-sets, all three parsers agreed on every 861-bit adjacency fingerprint, nauty-complg matched byte-for-byte, and nauty-labelg produced 656 distinct labels.
This does not establish that the 656 are all (5,5,42) graphs and does not change the bound 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
next_steps: Authenticate unchanged Springer Supplementary Data 4 with the same raw body/header/transfer discipline.
Only after the Springer gate passes, dual-check the two conflicts and canonicalize the valid K43 deletions against the authenticated supplied 656.
Then run one bounded six-vertex conflict-core repair discriminator; do not perform direct one-vertex extension of the known supplied controls.
experiments: .proof-experiments/20260720-221528-34af71: direct authoritative retrieval passed; HTTP 200, TLS verified, content_type null, 47,888 bytes, frozen SHA-256
.proof-experiments/20260720-221937-5c7218: provenance-v2/order-7 regression passed; four provenance mutations rejected and 1,044 graph classes plus complements agreed
.proof-experiments/20260720-221949-1e01b8: full supplied-656 corpus gate passed in 997.687 seconds
independent_checker: Checker A is Python with direct 5-subset combinations over a Boolean matrix. Checker B is separately parsed C with recursive bitset clique search. NetworkX supplies a third graph6 decoder; nauty-complg and nauty-labelg provide external complement and canonical-label paths. The new per-record manifest also exactly matches the prior host-side run.
transfer_insights: Fail-closed data provenance -> a missing response header must remain missing rather than be inferred -> schema v2 links raw headers and curl metadata and rejected four provenance mutations before admitting the corpus.

Isolated adversarial reconstruction -> correlated local graph transformations require an external representation -> target nauty-complg agreed byte-for-byte and NetworkX agreed on every complete adjacency fingerprint.

established_facts.claim: The directly retrieved supplied McKay packet has 328 valid source records whose complements yield 656 distinct supplied order-42 Ramsey graphs under the full local gate.
established_facts.scope: Exactly the supplied records and derived complements; not completeness

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Require or assert a text/plain response header for the current authoritative corpus response.
ruled_out.scope: The direct ANU HTTP/2 response captured on 2026-07-20
ruled_out.reason: The raw authoritative header block and curl transfer metadata both show Content-Type absent.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A later authoritative response actually supplies Content-Type; preserve it as a new response rather than rewriting this capture.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat 656 distinct canonical labels as an exhaustive classification of all order-42 Ramsey graphs.
ruled_out.reason: The gate checks only supplied records and contains no exhaustive generation or cover certificate.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Produce a replayable exhaustive enumeration and checked coverage certificate.

open_leads.rationale: It is the strongest published K43 repair seed and is now the remaining source gate before conflict-core work.
open_leads.next_experiment: Run the exact direct-HTTPS retrieval command in CHECKPOINT.md and preserve publisher body, raw headers, curl transfer metadata, and hashes.

open_leads.description: Execute the six-vertex published conflict-core repair slice after seed authentication.
open_leads.rationale: It is a sharply bounded exact neighborhood around the best sourced K43 near-solution.
open_leads.next_experiment: Freeze all edges outside the authenticated six-vertex conflict union and certify the bounded optimum under two encodings.

blocker: Springer Supplementary Data 4 remains unauthenticated; therefore no published-seed deletion novelty or conflict-core repair claim is yet admissible.
reopen_condition: Retrieve unchanged publisher bytes with raw provenance and obtain exact agreement under both graph checkers.
reopen_evidence: The prior McKay-corpus blocker is satisfied by .proof-experiments/20260720-221528-34af71 and artifacts/authenticated_corpus_report.json.

continuation.objective: Authenticate the exact publisher K43 two-conflict seed.
continuation.first_action: Run the Springer raw-TXT retrieval command in CHECKPOINT.md.
continuation.stop_condition: Stop on transport ambiguity, byte instability, parse ambiguity, or checker disagreement; do not begin repair before the gate passes.

candidate_profile.scholarly_question: Can the historically supplied (5,5,42) controls be admitted with fail-closed source provenance and independently replayed exact graph checks?
candidate_profile.meaningful_delta: The prior packet had matching bytes but inferred a missing Content-Type. This epoch retained a second direct body and immutable header/TLS capture, hardened the schema against inference, and completed the first admissible full supplied-656 gate.
candidate_profile.acceptance_test: A clean external replay from the retained raw body, header capture, sidecar, checker sources, and tool versions reproduces the decisive manifest and report fields.

closest_prior_work.difference: McKay supplies the historical graphs; this local artifact adds immutable transport provenance, multiple exact checker/parser paths, reference complementation, and a per-record manifest without claiming a new graph or classification.

novelty_searches.source: Dynamic Survey DS1.18 and McKay's maintained data page
novelty_searches.query: R(5,5) current bounds and order-42 graph corpus
novelty_searches.finding: The maintained range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46 and the 656 count remains strongly evidenced rather than proved complete.

external_channel.acceptance_path: Seek a clean third-party replay before considering maintainer contact; do not present this control artifact as a Ramsey-number result.

independent_validations.validator: Checker A, Checker B, NetworkX 3.3, nauty-complg, and nauty-labelg
independent_validations.result: All supplied 656 instances passed exact forbidden-set checks, representation checks, reference complementation, and distinct-label checks.

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol was principal investigator. Two precomputed GPT-5.6 Terra memos were advisory; Sol hash-verified them, relied on the experiment-verification memo only for identifying the provenance defect and next discriminator, and independently implemented and tested the fix. No new subagent was spawned. Deterministic tools: Python 3.12.3, C/GCC, curl 8.5.0/OpenSSL 3.0.13, NetworkX 3.3, nauty, SHA-256, Git diagnostics, and the computational-researcher experiment harness. Web browsing was used for the maintained survey and authoritative data pages. No SAT solver or proof assistant was used.
review_status: research-software progress; not a Ramsey result candidate
